title: wasm: JS JSON.parse failure for event silently delivers JsValue::NULL to callback
description: In client.rs set_event_callback (line 242-250), if js_sys::JSON::parse fails, the code falls back to JsValue::NULL and calls js_cb.call1(&JsValue::NULL, &JsValue::NULL). The JS callback receives null instead of an event object. A callback that reads event.type will throw a TypeError (cannot read property 'type' of null), which is caught by the if let Err(err) = js_cb.call1(...) on line 251 and logged to console.error — but the error message says nothing useful about which event was lost. This silently drops relay events with no indication to the caller. If JSON.parse can fail on well-formed serde_json output, it indicates a bug elsewhere; if it can't, the fallback is dead code. Either document that the fallback is unreachable, or propagate a typed error instead of NULL.
